title: D&D 5e 2024 Crafting Rules
description: Complete rules for crafting nonmagical items, potions, and spell scrolls

nonmagicalItems.description: To craft a nonmagical item, you need tools, raw materials, and time

tools.description: You must use the required tool to make an item and have proficiency with that tool. Anyone who helps you must also have proficiency with it.
tools.assignment: The DM assigns required tools for items not specifically listed in the Tools section.

rawMaterials.cost: To make an item, you need raw materials worth half its purchase cost (round down)
rawMaterials.example: You need 750 GP of raw materials to make Plate Armor, which sells for 1,500 GP
rawMaterials.availability: The DM determines whether appropriate raw materials are available

time.calculation: To determine how many days (working 8 hours a day) it takes to make an item, divide its purchase cost in GP by 10 (round a fraction up to a day)
time.example: You need 5 days to make a Heavy Crossbow, which sells for 50 GP
time.flexibility: If an item requires multiple days, the days needn't be consecutive

collaboration.description: Characters can combine their efforts to shorten the crafting time
collaboration.calculation: Divide the time needed to create an item by the number of characters working on it
collaboration.limitation: Normally, only one other character can assist you, but the DM might allow more assistants

potionsOfHealing.description: A character who has proficiency with the Herbalism Kit can create a Potion of Healing

spellScrolls.description: A spellcaster can transfer a spell to a scroll and create a Spell Scroll

prerequisites.skills: You must have proficiency in the Arcana skill or with Calligrapher's Supplies
prerequisites.spellPreparation: You must have the spell prepared on each day of the inscription
prerequisites.materialComponents: You must have at hand any Material components required by the spell; if the spell consumes its Material components, they are consumed only when you complete the scroll
prerequisites.spellParameters: The scroll's spell uses your spell save DC and spell attack bonus

cantrips.description: If the scribed spell is a cantrip, the version on the scroll works as if the caster were your level

spellScrolls.timeRequirement: For each day of inscription, you must work for 8 hours. If a scroll requires multiple days, those days needn't be consecutive.

generalRules.workingHours: Crafting requires 8 hours of work per day
generalRules.consecutiveDays: Days spent crafting need not be consecutive
generalRules.proficiencyRequirement: All participants in crafting must have proficiency with the required tools
generalRules.materialAvailability: The DM determines whether appropriate raw materials are available
generalRules.sellingCraftedItems: Crafted items can be used or sold at their normal price
